title: Quadrants on the coordinate plane
description: Practice identifying the quadrant or axis where points on the coordinate plane are located.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shows the four quadrants of the coordinate plane.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where is $\left(4,3\dfrac12\right)$ located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's graph $\left(\maroonD{4}, \tealE{3\dfrac{1}{2}}\right)$ by starting at the $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$. 

The $\maroonD{4}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\maroonD{4}$ units $\maroonD{\text{to the right}}$  of the origin.

[[☃ image 1]]

The $\tealE{3\dfrac{1}{2}}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\tealE{3\dfrac{1}{2}}$ units $\tealE{\text{above}}$ the $x$-axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

Since the $x$-coordinate is positive  and the $y$-coordinate is positive, our point is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{I}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\left(\maroonD{4}, \tealE{3\dfrac{1}{2}}\right)$ is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{I}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shows the four quadrants of the coordinate plane.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where is $(-1,5)$ located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's graph $(\maroonD{-1}, \tealE{5})$ by starting at the $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$. 

The $\maroonD{- 1}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\maroonD{1}$ unit $\maroonD{\text{to the left}}$  of the origin.

[[☃ image 1]]

The $\tealE{5}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\tealE{5}$ units $\tealE{\text{above}}$ the $x$-axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

Since the $x$-coordinate is negative  and the $y$-coordinate is positive, our point is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$(\maroonD{-1}, \tealE{5})$ is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where is $(-2.5,2)$ located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's graph $(\maroonD{-2.5}, \tealE{2})$ by starting at the $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$. 

The $\maroonD{- 2.5}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\maroonD{2.5}$ units $\maroonD{\text{to the left}}$  of the origin.

[[☃ image 1]]

The $\tealE{2}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\tealE{2}$ units $\tealE{\text{above}}$ the $x$-axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

Since the $x$-coordinate is negative and the $y$-coordinate is positive, our point is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$(\maroonD{-2.5}, \tealE{2})$ is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

**Where is $(-5,-9)$ located on the coordinate plane?**

hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

The $\tealE{-9}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\tealE{9}$ units $\tealE{\text{below}}$ the $x$-axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

Since the $x$-coordinate is negative and the $y$-coordinate is negative, our point is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{III}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where is $(6,0)$ located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's graph $(\maroonD{6}, \tealE{0})$ by starting at the $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$. 

The $\maroonD{6}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\maroonD{6}$ units $\maroonD{\text{to the right}}$  of the origin.

[[☃ image 1]]

The $\tealE{0}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is on the $x$-axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

Since the $x$-coordinate is positive and the $y$-coordinate is $0$, our point is located on the $x$-axis.

[[☃ image 1]]
$(\maroonD{6}, \tealE{0})$ is located on the $x$-axis.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where is $(0,4)$ located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

[[☃ image 2]]

Let's graph $(\maroonD{0}, \tealE{4})$ by starting at the $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$. 

The $\maroonD{0}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is on the $y$ axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

The $\tealE{4}$ in the ordered pair  tells us that our point is $\tealE{4}$ units $\tealE{\text{above}}$ the $x$-axis.

[[☃ image 2]]

Since the $x$-coordinate is $0$ and the $y$-coordinate is positive, our point is located on the $y$-axis.

[[☃ image 1]]
$(\maroonD{0}, \tealE{4})$ is located on the $y$-axis.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shows the four quadrants of the coordinate plane.

[[☃ image 1]]

Point $A$ is located at $(2, 4)$.

**Complete the statement.**

Point $A$ has an $x$-coordinate that is [[☃ dropdown 1]] and a $y$-coordinate that is [[☃ dropdown 2]], so the point is located [[☃ dropdown 3]].
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 2]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $A$ is located at $(\maroonD{2}, \tealE{4})$.

Its $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate, $\maroonD{2}$, is positive.

Its $\tealE{y}$-coordinate, $ \tealE{4}$, is positive.

Points in Quadrant $\mathrm{I}$ have positive $\maroonD{x}$- and $\tealE{y}$-coordinates.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $A$ has an $x$-coordinate that is positive and a $y$-coordinate that is positive, so the point is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{I}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

Its $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate, $\maroonD{-5}$, is negative.

Its $\tealE{y}$-coordinate, $ \tealE{7}$, is positive.

Points in Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$ have negative $\maroonD{x}$-coordinates and positive $\tealE{y}$-coordinates.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $A$ has an $x$-coordinate that is negative and a $y$-coordinate that is positive, so the point is located in Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shows the four quadrants of the coordinate plane.

[[☃ image 1]]

Point $A$ is located at $\left(0, -1\dfrac{1}{2}\right)$.

**Complete the statement.**

Point $A$ has an $x$-coordinate that is [[☃ dropdown 1]] and a $y$-coordinate that is [[☃ dropdown 2]], so the point is located [[☃ dropdown 3]].
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 1]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $A$ is located at $\left(\maroonD{0}, \tealE{-1\dfrac{1}{2}}\right)$.

Its $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate is zero.

Its $\tealE{y}$-coordinate, $ \tealE{-1\dfrac{1}{2}}$, is negative.

Points on the $y$-axis have an $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate of zero.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $A$ has an $x$-coordinate that is zero and $y$-coordinate that is negative, so the point is located on the $y$-axis.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shows the four quadrants of the coordinate plane.

[[☃ image 1]]

Point $A$ is located at $(-5, 0)$.

**Complete the statement.**

Point $A$ has an $x$-coordinate that is [[☃ dropdown 1]] and a $y$-coordinate that is [[☃ dropdown 2]], so the point is located [[☃ dropdown 3]].
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 1]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $A$ is located at $(\maroonD{-5}, \tealE{0})$.

Its $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate, $\maroonD{-5}$, is negative.

Its $\tealE{y}$-coordinate is zero.

Points on the $x$-axis have a $y$-coordinate of $0$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $A$ has an $x$-coordinate that is negative and a $y$-coordinate that is zero, so the point is located on the $x$-axis.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Point $E$ has a negative $x$-coordinate, and its $y$-coordinate is not $0$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where could point $E$ be located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 2]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $E$ has a negative $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate. This means it must be *left* of the $y$-axis.

This means point $E$ can only be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{II}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{III}}$, or on the $\purpleD{\text{negative }x \text{-axis}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Point $E$'s $\tealE{y}$-coordinate is *not* $0$, so its $\tealE{y}$-coordinate must be either positive or negative. 

This means Point $E$ *cannot* be on the $x$-axis, but could be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{II}}$ or $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{III}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $E$ could be located in Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$ or Quadrant $\mathrm{III}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Point $E$ has an $x$-coordinate that is not $0$, and its $y$-coordinate is negative.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where could point $E$ be located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 1]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $E$'s $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate is *not* $0$, so its $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate must be either positive or negative. 

This means Point $E$ *cannot* be on the $y$-axis, but could be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{I}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{II}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{III}}$,  $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{IV}}$, or on the $\purpleD{x \text{-axis}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Point $E$ has a negative $\tealE{y}$-coordinate. This means it must be *below* the $x$-axis.

This means point $E$ can only be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{III}}$ or $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{IV}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $E$ could be located in Quadrant $\mathrm{III}$ or Quadrant $\mathrm{IV}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Point $E$ has a positive $y$-coordinate.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where could point $E$ be located on the coordinate plane?**


[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 1]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $E$ has a positive $\tealE{y}$-coordinate. This means it must be *above* the $x$-axis.

This means point $E$ can only be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{I}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{II}}$, or on the $\purpleD{\text{positive } y \text{-axis}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $E$ could be located in Quadrant $\mathrm{I}$, Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$, or on the $y$-axis.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Point $E\text{'s}$ $x$- and $y$-coordinates have different signs (one is positive and the other is negative).  Neither coordinate is $0$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where could point $E$ be located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 1]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $E\text{'s}$ $\maroonD{x}$- and $\tealE{y}$-coordinates have different signs. One is positive and the other is negative; neither is $0$.

This means Point $E$ *cannot* be on the $x$- or $y$-axis, but could be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{II}}$ or $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{IV}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $E$ could be located in  Quadrant $\mathrm{II}$ or Quadrant $\mathrm{IV}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Point $E\text{'s}$ $y$-coordinate is $0$, but its $x$-coordinate is not $0$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where could point $E$ be located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 1]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $E$'s $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate is *not* $0$, so its $\maroonD{x}$-coordinate must be either positive or negative.

This means point $E$ *cannot* be on the $y$-axis, but could be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{I}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{II}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{III}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{IV}}$ or on the $\purpleD{ x\text{-axis}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Point $E$ has a $\tealE{y}$-coordinate that is $\tealE0$. This means it must be on the $x$-axis.

This means point $E$ can only be on the $\purpleD{ x \text{-axis}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $E$ is located on the $x$-axis.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where could point $E$ be located on the coordinate plane?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 2]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $E$ has a positive $\maroonE{x}$-coordinate. This means it must be to the *right* of the $y$-axis.

This means point $E$ can only be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{I}}$, $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{IV}}$, or on the $\purpleD{\text{positive } x \text{-axis}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $E$ could be located in Quadrant $\mathrm{I}$, Quadrant $\mathrm{IV}$, or on the $x$-axis.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Point $E\text{'s}$ $x$- and $y$-coordinates have the same sign. Neither coordinate is $0$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Where could point $E$ be located on the coordinate plane?**


[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The $x$- and $y$-axes divide the coordinate plane into four quadrants.

[[☃ image 1]]

Any point on the coordinate plane is in one of the quadrants or on an axis. The $\blueE{\text{origin}}$, $(0, 0)$, is at the intersection of the two axes.

Point $E\text{'s}$ $\maroonD{x}$- and $\tealE{y}$-coordinates have the same signs. Both are positive or both are negative; neither is $0$.

This means Point $E$ *cannot* be on the $x$- or $y$-axis, but could be in $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{I}}$ or $\purpleD{\text{Quadrant }\mathrm{III}}$.

[[☃ image 2]]
Point $E$ could be located in Quadrant $\mathrm{I}$ or Quadrant $\mathrm{III}$.

options.alt: An XY coordinate plane divided into four quadrants. The x axis runs from left to right.  The y axis runs up and down. Quadrant 1 is the top right quadrant. Quadrant 2 is the top left quadrant. Quadrant 3 is the bottom left quadrant. Quadrant 4 is the bottom right quadrant.
